title: The One With the Two Bullies

expressions.dialogue: No because **I chickened out** the last time when I tried to meet him.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 두려움 때문에 계획했던 일을 실행하지 못할 때 쓰는 구어적 표현. 자신이나 타인의 소심함을 가볍게 놀리는 뉘앙스가 있다.
expressions.example: I was going to ask her out, but I chickened out at the last second. → 고백하려고 했는데 마지막 순간에 겁먹고 포기했어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Well, he did this bit... You probably had to be there, but it was Liza Minelli locked in our freezer, eating a raw chicken. (Phoebe bursts out in a laugh)

expressions.dialogue: Man, **I am so beat**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 구어체에서 'exhausted'보다 캐주얼하고 직접적인 표현. 신체적 피로뿐 아니라 정신적 피로에도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: After that three-hour meeting, I'm so beat I can barely keep my eyes open. → 세 시간짜리 회의 끝에 완전히 녹초야. 눈도 못 뜨겠어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: No! Listen, I'm not gonna go through this with you again, okay. Just once I wanna beat when you can't blame it on the broken nose, the buzzer, or the fact that you thought you were getting mono. Let's just call this, tie score and it's halftime.

occ.speaker: Pete
occ.text: I can't until I'm the ultimate fighter. I will do it. I'm telling you, the day will come when children will argue over who will win a fight, me or Superman. Now, I'm not saying I could beat Superman, but y'know, kids are stupid.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I'm kinda beat.

expressions.dialogue: Hey, you just wanna forget about racquetball and **hang out here**?
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 특별한 목적 없이 편하게 시간을 보내다
expressions.nuance: 친구들 사이에서 가장 자주 쓰이는 표현 중 하나. 'meet up'보다 더 느긋하고 계획 없는 뉘앙스를 담는다.
expressions.example: We didn't end up going to the game. We just hung out at Mike's place all afternoon. → 결국 경기는 못 갔고 오후 내내 마이크 집에서 그냥 놀았어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Well actually thanks, but I think I'm just gonna hang out here tonight.  It's been kinda a long day.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: You guys are always hanging out in my apartment! Come on, I'll only use my left hand, huh? Come on, wussies! (Joey and Chandler pick her up) All right, ok, I gotta go. I'm going, (they throw her out) and I'm gone.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: (as Ursula) If it was, would you stop hanging out with her?

expressions.dialogue: No actually, **I was just going for colorful**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 'I was trying to be funny'보다 더 구체적인 의도를 드러내는 표현. 유머, 패션, 예술 등 다양한 맥락에서 자연스럽게 쓰인다.
expressions.example: The director wasn't going for realistic—he was going for dreamlike. → 감독은 사실적인 느낌을 노린 게 아니라 몽환적인 분위기를 의도했어.

occ.speaker: Barry
occ.text: Yeah! It's fine, it's fine. Robbie's gonna be here for hours.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: (leaving to serve others) Good for me!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Okay, okay. If I were omnipotent for a day, I would want, um, world peace, no more hunger, good things for the rain-forest...And bigger boobs!

expressions.dialogue: **What's with this guy?**
expressions.speaker: Big Bully
expressions.meaning: 저 사람 왜 저래? / 저게 뭐야?
expressions.nuance: 누군가의 이상한 행동이나 태도에 의아함을 표현하는 구어체. 약간 짜증스럽거나 어이없다는 뉘앙스가 섞여 있다.
expressions.example: What's with her today? She's been acting weird ever since lunch. → 걔 오늘 왜 저래? 점심 먹고 나서부터 계속 이상하게 굴잖아.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: See ya.... Waitwait, what's with you?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (trying desperately to change the subject) So, Ross, what's going on with you? Any stories? (Digs her elbow into his hand.) No news, no little anecdotes to share with the folks?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: What's going on with you?

expressions.dialogue: No, **just wanna make sure we're on the same page**.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 서로 같은 이해나 인식을 공유하고 있다
expressions.nuance: 오해를 방지하거나 확인할 때 쓰이는 표현. Chandler는 불량배들과 상황을 아이러니하게 '확인'하는 용도로 쓴다.
expressions.example: Before we start the project, let's make sure we're all on the same page about the timeline. → 프로젝트 시작 전에, 일정에 대해 다 같이 이해가 맞는지 확인하자.

expressions.dialogue: **It bit.** It was a 50's theme restaurant.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 'It sucked'의 1990년대 구어체 동의어. 현재는 sucked가 더 일반적이지만 같은 상황에서 자연스럽게 이해된다.
expressions.example: The concert was supposed to be amazing, but honestly? It bit. The sound was terrible. → 콘서트가 엄청날 거라 했는데, 솔직히? 최악이었어. 음향이 엉망이었거든.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh, you wouldn't know a great butt if it came up and bit ya.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Ooh, do I sense a little bit of resentment?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I'm taking my time, alright? I'm laying the groundwork. Yeah. I mean, every day I get just a little bit closer to...

expressions.expression: not know the first thing about [something]
expressions.dialogue: Monica, what are you talking about? **You don't know the first thing about the stock market.**
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 'know nothing'보다 강조된 표현으로, 어떤 분야에 완전히 무지하다는 것을 직접적으로 지적할 때 쓴다. 약간 비판적인 어조.
expressions.example: He's trying to fix his own car, but he doesn't know the first thing about engines. → 그는 직접 차를 고치려 하는데, 엔진에 대해 아무것도 몰라.

expressions.dialogue: Oh God, **here we go.** For the first time in my life I'm gonna say 'Hi birthfather'.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 긴장되거나 어려운 상황을 앞두고 스스로 다짐하거나 체념하듯 말할 때 쓴다. 맥락에 따라 설렘·불안·체념 등 다양한 감정을 담을 수 있다.
expressions.example: Deep breath. Here we go. → 숨 한번 크게 쉬고. 자, 간다.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Here we go.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Hi. Um, I want to start with a song thats about that moment when you suddenly realize what life is all about. OK, here we go. (plays a chord, then the lights go out) OK, thank you very much.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Ok, ok, here we go. (he crouches down near her stomach) Ok, where am I talking to, here? I mean, uh, well, there is one way that seems to offer a certain acoustical advantage, but...

expressions.dialogue: Ok, **get the hell off** my leg you yippity piece of crap.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 'hell'이 강조어로 삽입된 구조. 'get off'보다 훨씬 강한 분노나 절박함을 나타낸다. 격식 없는 상황에서만 사용 가능.
expressions.example: Get the hell out of my office! → 당장 내 사무실에서 꺼져!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: C'mon, you're going out with the guy! There's gotta be something wrong with him!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I think for us, kissing is pretty much like an opening act, y'know? I mean it's like the stand-up comedian you have to sit through before Pink Floyd comes out.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: (carrying a tray of drinks) Alright, don't tell me, don't tell me! (Starts handing them out.) Decaf cappucino for Joey.. Coffee black.. Late.. And an iced tea. I'm getting pretty good at this!

expressions.dialogue: Alright, that's it. **I've had enough of this**, alright.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 이제 더는 못 참겠다, 진절머리가 난다
expressions.nuance: 오랜 인내 끝에 폭발하거나 결단을 내릴 때 쓰는 표현. 'That's it'과 함께 자주 등장한다.
expressions.example: I've had enough of his excuses. I'm not covering for him anymore. → 그의 변명은 이제 신물이 나. 더 이상 그를 위해 변명해주지 않을 거야.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Y'know what? I-I-I... I-I have had enough of this! Y'know, I-I-I care a great deal about your daughter and I have treated her with nothing but respect! So if-if you've got a problem with me, frankly...

expressions.dialogue: Alright, **let's take this outside.**
expressions.speaker: Big Bully
expressions.nuance: 공개적인 장소에서의 충돌을 피하고 밖에서 해결하자고 할 때 쓰는 전형적인 표현. 영화와 드라마에서 싸움 도발의 클리셰로 자주 등장한다.
expressions.example: The argument got heated, and one of them said, 'You wanna handle this? Let's take it outside.' → 말다툼이 격해지자 한 명이 말했다. '이거 해결하고 싶어? 밖에서 하자.'

expressions.dialogue: **You had to ask.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 그걸 물어봐야 했어? (물어봐서 이 꼴이 됐잖아)
expressions.nuance: 위험하거나 어리석은 질문을 한 결과가 바로 나타날 때 체념하듯 하는 말. 한국어로 '괜히 물어봤다'는 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: 'Why is everyone staring at us?' 'You had to ask.' Just then, the fire alarm went off. → '왜 다들 우리 쳐다보는 거야?' '괜히 물어봤어.' 그 순간 화재경보기가 울렸다.

occ.speaker: Luisa
occ.text: Ah, it's not so much you, you were fat, you had your own problems. (To Rachel) But you? What a bitch!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Ok, uh, hey Richard, if you had an extra ticket to the Knicks game and you had to choose between a friend who smells and one who bruises you who would you pick?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: So, if you're parents hadn't got divorced, you'd be able to answer a question like a normal person?

expressions.dialogue: I say you and I go back down there and **stand up to those guys**.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 강자나 위협적인 상대에게 물러서지 않고 맞설 때 쓰는 표현. 용기 있는 행동을 묘사할 때 긍정적인 뉘앙스로 많이 쓰인다.
expressions.example: It took a lot of courage for her to stand up to her boss in front of everyone. → 모든 사람 앞에서 상사에게 맞서는 건 엄청난 용기가 필요했다.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I think for us, kissing is pretty much like an opening act, y'know? I mean it's like the stand-up comedian you have to sit through before Pink Floyd comes out.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh. Oooh! Ooh! Ooh! (She stands up and starts to dance around) Ooh! My butt cheek is waking up! Oooh! Ooh!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: (standing up) Uh, I like to, uh, to add something to that...

expressions.dialogue: Alright, **hang on a second** there, Custer.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 'wait'보다 더 구어적이고 자연스러운 표현. 상대를 멈추게 하거나 생각할 시간이 필요할 때 쓴다. 'Custer'는 유머러스한 별명으로 덧붙인 것.
expressions.example: Hang on a second—did you just say you quit your job? → 잠깐만, 방금 직장 그만뒀다고 했어?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Hang on a second I just got in.

occ.speaker: Carol
occ.text: (running over and grabbing the phone away from Ross) (on phone) Phoebe, hang on a second. (Hands Ross her keys) Here, take my car, go pick up your friends.

occ.speaker: Pete
occ.text: Hang on a second. (to the employees) I'll-I'll talk to you in the morning. (turns two of the three off) I'm sorry what?

expressions.dialogue: maybe that's just something every man has to go through once in his life, ya know, like a **right of passage** or somethin'.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 통과의례, 인생에서 한 번쯤 겪어야 하는 경험
expressions.nuance: 원래 'rite of passage'가 맞지만 Ross는 'right of passage'로 잘못 말한다—캐릭터의 자연스러운 실수. 어떤 나이나 단계에서 필수적으로 거치는 경험을 묘사한다.
expressions.example: Getting your heart broken for the first time is practically a rite of passage. → 처음으로 실연을 당하는 건 사실상 통과의례야.

expressions.dialogue: Hey, **I've come to terms with it**, you have to too.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 단순히 'accept'보다 감정적인 과정을 거쳐 받아들이게 됐다는 뉘앙스. 슬픔·상실·실패 등 부정적인 상황에 주로 쓰인다.
expressions.example: It took him years to come to terms with his father's death. → 그가 아버지의 죽음을 받아들이는 데 몇 년이 걸렸다.

expressions.dialogue: He left four years ago so **we're expecting him back any minute now.**
expressions.speaker: Mrs. Buffay
expressions.nuance: 문자 그대로는 '곧 돌아올 것 같다'는 뜻이지만, 4년째 돌아오지 않는 남편에 대한 씁쓸한 사르카즘으로 쓰인다. 영어 풍자 표현의 전형적인 활용.
expressions.example: He said he'd be right back, and that was an hour ago. I'm sure he'll be back any minute now. → 금방 돌아온다고 했는데 한 시간이 지났어. 이제 곧 오겠지, 분명히. (비꼼)

expressions.expression: I don't know what to do with that
expressions.dialogue: **I don't know what to do with that.**
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 그 정보를 어떻게 받아들여야 할지 모르겠다
expressions.nuance: 예상치 못한 정보나 감당하기 어려운 내용을 들었을 때 어떻게 반응해야 할지 모를 때 쓴다. 당혹감, 어이없음, 혹은 억제된 감동을 나타낼 수 있다.
expressions.example: 'He said he still thinks about her every day.' 'Yeah... I don't know what to do with that.' → '아직도 매일 그녀 생각을 한대.' '그래... 그걸 어떻게 받아들여야 할지 모르겠네.'

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: (she's wearing an oven mitt to protect her hand) I give up you guys, I don't know what I'm going to do with this thing!

expressions.dialogue: Yes, we're **standing our ground**...apparently.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 자신의 입장이나 자리를 고수하다, 물러서지 않다
expressions.nuance: 압박을 받아도 입장을 양보하지 않는다는 의미. Chandler는 자신도 모르게 상황에 끌려갔다는 자조('apparently')를 얹어 웃음을 유발한다.
expressions.example: Even when everyone disagreed with her, she stood her ground and turned out to be right. → 모두가 반대했을 때도 그녀는 입장을 굽히지 않았고, 결국 옳은 것으로 드러났다.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: You're damn right I'm right. I say you show this guy what you're made of. I say you stand your ground. I say you show him that you are the baddest hombre west of the lingerie.

expressions.dialogue: Man you really, bing, **gave it to** old Mr. Clean back there.
expressions.speaker: Little Bully
expressions.nuance: 싸움에서 상대를 이겼거나 논쟁에서 압도한 상황을 묘사하는 구어적 표현. 'Mr. Clean'은 대머리 남자를 유머러스하게 부르는 별명.
expressions.example: Did you see that? She really gave it to him in the argument. He had nothing to say back. → 봤어? 논쟁에서 그녀가 그를 제대로 눌렀어. 그는 아무 말도 못했어.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Alright.. okay. No. Give it to me.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Oh wait, I have the ten of spades! Here! (gives it to Phoebe)

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: (picks it up) OK... ah, it's light... (shakes it)...it rattles... it's... (opens it) Travel Scrabble! Oooohhh, thank you! (she gives it back to him)

expressions.dialogue: **Don't do that to yourself.** Any one of us could have tripped over that little girl's jump-rope.
expressions.speaker: Big Bully
expressions.meaning: 자책하지 마, 스스로 너무 괴롭히지 마
expressions.nuance: 상대방이 자책하거나 죄책감을 느낄 때 위로하는 표현. 'Don't blame yourself'보다 더 자연스럽고 구어적이다.
expressions.example: 'I keep thinking I should have called him that night.' 'Hey, don't do that to yourself. You couldn't have known.' → '그날 밤 그에게 전화했어야 했는데.' '야, 자책하지 마. 알 수 없었잖아.'

expressions.dialogue: my motto is **get out before they go down**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 떨어지기 전에 빠져나오다, 손해 보기 전에 발을 빼다
expressions.nuance: 주식 투자에서 나온 말이지만 관계나 프로젝트 등 다양한 상황에 적용 가능하다. 직관적인 생활 철학처럼 들린다.
expressions.example: I sold all my shares last week. My motto: get out before they go down. → 지난 주에 주식 다 팔았어. 내 원칙: 떨어지기 전에 빠져나와야 해.

expressions.dialogue: I want you to know that he'll be returned to you, **almost as good as new**, within 24 hours.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 거의 새것이나 다름없이 / (아이러니) 상당히 다쳤지만 그래도 멀쩡하다는 식으로
expressions.nuance: 원래는 수리나 세탁 후 상태가 좋다는 표현이지만, 여기서는 귀를 다쳐 8바늘이나 꿰맨 개를 '거의 새것'이라고 표현해 아이러니한 웃음을 자아낸다.
expressions.example: The mechanic said it's almost as good as new—right before telling me it needs four more parts. → 정비사가 거의 새것이나 다름없다고 했어—부품 네 개가 더 필요하다고 말하기 직전에.

expressions.dialogue: **On behalf of everyone**, I'd just like to say behuh.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 공식적인 발표나 연설에서 쓰이는 격식체 표현인데, Ross는 이를 Phoebe의 황당한 징조 이야기에 과장되게 사용해 유머를 만든다. 아이러니로도 자주 활용된다.
expressions.example: On behalf of everyone here, I'd like to thank you for all your hard work this year. → 여기 있는 모두를 대표해, 올 한 해 수고하신 것에 감사드립니다.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Yeah, from now on everyone you lurk, I'm gonna lurk first! You move on to someone else, I'm gonna be one step ahead of you, every single time! And then I'll be on your ass every hour of every day 'til Monday, because that's when I go home. When do you leave?
